pattern_recognition: This problem falls under the category of stack-based problems, specifically dealing with balanced parentheses. The signals include the need to match opening and closing brackets while maintaining the correct order.

approaches.title: Brute Force
approaches.intuition: Check all possible combinations of parentheses to see if they can be arranged correctly.
approaches.algorithm: Iterate through the string and use recursion to generate all combinations of brackets, checking if each combination is valid.

approaches.title: Optimal Approach
approaches.intuition: Utilize a stack to keep track of opening brackets and ensure they are closed in the correct order.
approaches.algorithm: Traverse the string, pushing opening brackets onto the stack and popping them when a matching closing bracket is encountered. At the end, the stack should be empty if the string is valid.

dry_run: For the input '()[]{}': Start with an empty stack. Read '(': push onto stack. Read ')': pop from stack (valid). Read '[': push onto stack. Read ']': pop from stack (valid). Read '{': push onto stack. Read '}': pop from stack (valid). Stack is empty at the end, so the result is true.
edge_cases: An empty input string is valid. A single element like '(' or ')' is invalid. Duplicates like '((()))' are valid. Large inputs up to 10^4 characters should be handled efficiently. Overflow is not a concern as we are only dealing with characters.
common_pitfalls: Common mistakes include: 1) Not using a stack, leading to incorrect order checking. 2) Forgetting to check for mismatched types of brackets, like '([)]'. 3) Not handling empty strings correctly, assuming they are invalid.
